mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals tightening labor markets, increasing corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.3 million units combined with rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 50%+ of instances where both HOUST and INDPRO exceed thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 104.0 signals stronger supply-side capacity utilization, reducing inflationary pressures and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds 104.0.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3.5% triggers expectations of monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP growth (GDPC1) signals weakening demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 50%+ of instances where both RSAFS and GDPC1 fall below thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries, a 3-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 160,000 signals strengthening labor markets, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 50%+ of post-recession periods with PAYEMS above 160,000.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in consumer confidence (RSAFS) combined with falling housing starts (HOUST) indicates deteriorating demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST fall below thresholds.
